Who I Am

About Me

I am a results-driven MBA candidate specializing in Finance and Human Resource Management, backed by a strong academic foundation in Electrical and Electronics Engineering with a CGPA of 9.3 and Distinction.

My unique profile combines technical depth — gained through engineering, IoT projects, and a year-long substation internship — with strategic business thinking developed during my MBA. I apply analytical precision to financial planning, HR operations, and cross-functional problem solving.

I thrive at the intersection of technology and business, translating complex engineering logic into operational and financial insights that drive real organizational value.

Industry Exposure

Work Experience

⚡
Electrical Engineering Intern
Substation Parvathipuram, Nagercoil
Jul 2023 – Jul 2024 · 1 Year
  • Gained hands-on exposure to transformer operation, maintenance procedures, and fault identification in a live substation environment
  • Studied implementation and functioning of lightning arrestors for high-voltage electrical safety protocols
  • Assisted in monitoring control building operations, battery backup systems, and switchgear management
  • Developed working knowledge of three-phase power transmission, distribution infrastructure, and load balancing principles

Academic Work

Key Projects

IoT-Based Three-Phase Power Failure Monitoring & DG Interlock System
B.E. Final Year Project · 2024–2025
IoT GSM/SMS Module Microcontroller Three-Phase Sensing Voice Announcement Module Real-Time Monitoring
  • Designed and built a real-time monitoring system to detect three-phase power failures and trigger automated DG interlock switching
  • Integrated SMS alert functionality to notify concerned personnel instantly upon power fault detection
  • Incorporated a voice announcement module to provide on-site audio alerts for improved response time and safety
  • Combined IoT-based remote monitoring with local control logic to ensure uninterrupted power supply management
